---
title: "Assessment & Grading"
page-layout: full
---

## Grading Scheme (Scheme B: Specifications Grading)

| Component | Weight |
|-----------|--------|
| **Projects (2)** | 55% |
| **Homeworks** | 30% |
| **Checkpoints (3)** | 9% |
| **Peer-Engagement Loops** | 5% |
| **Prerequisite Quiz** | 1% |

**Specifications Grading**: Work is evaluated on a **pass/revise** basis rather than point accumulation. Each assessment bundle (projects, homeworks, checkpoints) has a defined specification; work meeting the spec earns the weighted points. Work not meeting the spec may be revised and resubmitted within a grace period.

---

## Projects (55% of grade)

Two integrative projects replace traditional midterm and final exams. Each project synthesizes multiple weeks of the course arc.

### Project 1: "One Problem, Three Lenses"
*Due Week 5 (TODO — calendar TBD)*

Use a single regression problem with a clinical dataset. Solve it three ways:
- **Least Squares**: classical normal equations and interpretation
- **Maximum Likelihood Estimation**: Gaussian model, negative log-likelihood
- **Bayesian**: posterior inference and credible intervals

Synthesis question: How do the three approaches diverge and converge? What assumptions differ?

### Project 2: "From Inference to Discovery"
*Due Week 7 (TODO — calendar TBD)*

Same dataset, now tasked with discovering structure:
- **Approximate Inference**: variational inference or sampling
- **Discriminative Classification**: logistic regression or SVM
- **Unsupervised Learning**: k-means clustering + PCA for dimensionality reduction

Synthesis question: What story does the data tell when you stop presuming its structure?

**Submission**: GitHub Classroom. See [GitHub Classroom]({{< var classroom_url >}}) for the invite link.

---

## Homeworks (30% of grade)

Short problem sets (1–2 per week) emphasizing computation and interpretation. Deployed via **GitHub Classroom** — all submissions must be pushed to your private repo. Homeworks are practice-graded on a pass/revise basis; three passes earn full homework credit.

The [Homeworks repository]({{< var homeworks_repo >}}) contains specifications and autograder fixtures. No solution content is visible here; only you and Greg can see your repo.

---

## Checkpoints (9% of grade)

Three retakeable in-class assessments (Weeks 2, 4, 6) covering:
- **Checkpoint 1 (Week 2)**: Linear regression, bias–variance, regularization
- **Checkpoint 2 (Week 4)**: Classification, likelihood, Bayesian inference
- **Checkpoint 3 (Week 6)**: Unsupervised learning, dimensionality reduction

Each checkpoint is offered twice (original + retake within 1 week). You keep your best score. Passing all three checkpoints earns the 9% weighting.

**Dates**: Scheduled during class time. TODO — calendar pending.

---

## Peer-Engagement Loops (5% of grade)

Six weekly loops (Weeks 1–6): complete the interactive tool, generate your token, post to D2L, review peers, integrate feedback. Completing all six loops earns 5%.

See [Activities](activities.qmd) for loop details.

---

## Prerequisite Quiz (1% of grade)

A brief assessment (administered Week 1) on linear algebra and probability fundamentals. Pass = 1% credit. It's primarily diagnostic; most students pass.

---

## Revision & Grace Policies

All work (projects, homeworks, checkpoints) can be revised. You have **3 days after feedback** to resubmit with revisions. The goal is mastery, not perfection on first attempt.
